methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Zap Energy

data.narrative:
Radiant, a Series D nuclear energy company founded by Doug Bernauer, has raised $465M+ to date. The valuation universe includes 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 5 private peers (Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, Zap Energy), though the narrow peer set constrains traditional multiples-based analysis.

Stage-progression modeling, anchored to Radiant's last post-money valuation of $1.0B, applies a 1.5x multiplier typical for Series D advancement, yielding $1.5B. A peer-median approach is not applicable; while 4 same-sector peers exist, none met the narrow filters for stage, adjacency, or valuation tier required for reliable calibration. Broadening to all-sector comparables would introduce unacceptable sector basis risk.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, with a median of $1.5B. This range reflects high sensitivity to two key uncertainties: the absence of sufficient narrow-peer transactions limits confidence in step-up assumptions, and sector-specific maturation dynamics in advanced nuclear remain nascent. The $1.5B median should be treated as indicative pending closer review of recent financing terms among the identified private peer set and any emerging Series D transactions in the nuclear subsector.
